pulse: The consumer sector currently trades at a median EV/EBITDA of 15.1x and a median P/E of 26.8x, which are roughly in line with the broader market multiples. Notably, Best Buy (BBY) has reported disappointing holiday sales despite showing a profit margin of 2.6%, highlighting the ongoing pressure on consumer discretionary spending amidst a backdrop of stagnant retail sales growth reflected in headlines such as "U.S. Retail Sales Unexpectedly Flat in December." Moving forward, investors should brace for potential volatility as consumer sentiment remains fragile, with softening demand indicators suggesting that caution may be warranted in stock selection within the space.
